input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Enforce an outbound web proxy (deploy Squid) and require all HTTP(S) egress to flow through it; enable HTTPS interception (SSL/TLS inspection) and configure the proxy to block or reject HTTP methods and request bodies used for file uploads (e.g., block/limit POST multipart uploads and large request bodies), apply content-type and upload-size limits, and allow only GET/HEAD for general internet access so exfiltration via HTTPS is prevented while permitting normal outbound web access.", "Place a Snort IPS inline on the LAN side (between the firewall and internal hosts) to detect and block exfiltration patterns (large multipart POSTs, archive uploads, anomalous C2-style HTTPS traffic and known exfil signatures) and alert on host-to-internet connections that match data-exfil patterns for rapid response.", "Disable or remove common command-line upload utilities and interactive network tools on the compromised host (render curl/wget/netcat/socat non-executable or remove them) to prevent easy use of those binaries for exfiltration while leaving required agent processes intact.", "Harden the host with application control / allowlisting for user shells and administrative utilities (implement application whitelisting or restrict execution to a known good set of binaries for non-system services), and enable mandatory access controls (AppArmor/SELinux) to confine processes so even a privileged interactive shell has constrained file/network capabilities.", "Enforce egress policy at the firewall to require proxy usage for web traffic (only allow outbound HTTP(S) via the internal proxy), restrict direct outbound HTTP(S) from endpoints, and limit DNS resolution to internal resolvers to reduce covert channels and make monitoring/inspection effective."], "chosen_mitigation": "Enforce an outbound web proxy (deploy Squid) and require all HTTP(S) egress to flow through it; enable HTTPS interception (SSL/TLS inspection) and configure the proxy to block or reject HTTP methods and request bodies used for file uploads (e.g., block/limit POST multipart uploads and large request bodies), apply content-type and upload-size limits, and allow only GET/HEAD for general internet access so exfiltration via HTTPS is prevented while permitting normal outbound web access.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 1, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2026-03-21T19:56:47.577059"}

{"suggestions": ["Enable FortiGate SSL/TLS deep inspection and configure the FortiGate DLP/IPS/Web-Filtering features to block technique-level exfiltration: detect and block HTTP(S) multipart/form-data uploads, requests with large Content-Length bodies, archive/mime-type signatures (e.g., .tar, .gz, application/x-gzip), and file-upload HTTP methods (POST/PUT) from endpoints while still permitting benign GET/HEAD traffic. Keep rules generic to the technique (upload patterns, large payloads, archive signatures) rather than blocking specific destinations.", "Deploy host-based real-time file change monitoring (auditd/inotify or equivalent) on endpoints to detect creation of staging folders and compressed archives (e.g., new staged directories, *.tar, *.gz). Feed these alerts to SOC tooling and an orchestration action that dynamically reduces that host's allowed outbound methods (e.g., restrict POST/PUT for that host) and increases logging/packet capture for investigation \u2014 without isolating or disrupting the host process running splunkd.", "Apply egress session size and rate-limiting on the perimeter firewall (FortiGate): set per-session byte/throughput caps and shorter session timeouts for outbound web sessions from endpoints to prevent bulk exfiltration over a single connection and make large uploads fail or be slow enough to be detected and investigated.", "Harden endpoint auditing and command-line/process monitoring: enable comprehensive process command-line logging and file-access auditing for privileged processes so security analysts get immediate visibility when data-collection utilities (find, tar, gzip, cp) are used to read many files or create archives; tune alerts for suspicious patterns (mass file reads + compression) to trigger containment workflows that preserve splunkd connectivity.", "Implement network-based behavior detection signatures on the FortiGate (application control / IPS rules) to flag unusual web-client behavior (e.g., web clients issuing repeated multipart uploads or anomalous large outbound POSTs), and send high-priority alerts to incident response teams to enact manual defenses while maintaining required agent heartbeat traffic."], "chosen_mitigation": "Enable FortiGate SSL/TLS deep inspection and configure the FortiGate DLP/IPS/Web-Filtering features to block technique-level exfiltration: detect and block HTTP(S) multipart/form-data uploads, requests with large Content-Length bodies, archive/mime-type signatures (e.g., .tar, .gz, application/x-gzip), and file-upload HTTP methods (POST/PUT) from endpoints while still permitting benign GET/HEAD traffic. Keep rules generic to the technique (upload patterns, large payloads, archive signatures) rather than blocking specific destinations.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 2, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2026-03-21T20:11:44.059305"}
